preliminary_verdict.reason: SECURITY_FORCED under rigid policy §4.1: public_exploit_available=true (the advisory ships a working OOM DoS PoC). Policy §4.1 is disjunctive and non-negotiable — a public PoC forces SECURITY_FORCED regardless of reachability; it cannot be downgraded because ws is test-only. Advisory GHSA-96hv-2xvq-fx4p (CVE-2026-48779, OOM DoS) is fixed in 8.21.0; target equals the fixing version, so the finding is remediated. Release age 1448.3h clears cooldown; SECURITY_FORCED bypasses cooldown anyway. ws is transitive dev/test-only via happy-dom@20.8.4 (range ^8.18.3 satisfied) and is not runtime-reachable in production, which bounds blast radius but does not change the forced verdict. Pinned to exactly 8.21.0 via root override (not 8.21.1) per operator cooldown discipline. Remediated SECURITY_FORCED retains the post-deploy watch.
